# Perspective restore into changed topology: reconciliation + the fail-closed contract

## Context
Tree line **B5** (#13158 map) — the HARD half of restore (fable-tier in the triage): applying a captured perspective when the window topology CHANGED (fewer/more windows than capture time). ADR 0029 §2.2's restore-semantics table governs; #14653 owns the unchanged-topology case.

## The Problem
Changed-topology restore is where designs lie: silently dropping panes, spawning surprise windows, or half-applying are all worse than refusing. §2.2's contract: deterministic reconciliation for the coverable subset + an explicit, RENDERED fail-closed result for what cannot restore (the Demo-B scene G4 shows this honestly — it is a feature, not an apology).

## The Architectural Reality
Reconciliation = mapping captured window-slots onto live windows by shape affinity (never ids), then per-window #14653 restore plans; the uncoverable remainder returns a structured `unrestored` set (items + reason classes). No window CREATION in v1 (spawning is a §2.3/#13028-boundary decision — out of scope, documented). Blocked_by #14653 + B3.

## The Fix
One PR: the slot-mapping reconciler (pure) + composed restore + the fail-closed result shape + specs (shrunk topology: best-coverage mapping + unrestored set; grown topology: no surprise spawns; determinism).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Slot mapping is deterministic + id-free (spec).
- [ ] Unrestored set carries reason classes; NOTHING silently drops (spec: item count conservation — restored + unrestored = captured).
- [ ] No window creation in v1 (review-checkable).
- [ ] Composes #14653 per-window (no duplicate restore logic).
- [ ] Cross-family review.

## Out of Scope
Window spawning · unchanged-topology path (#14653) · UI rendering of the fail-closed result (Demo-B G4 + B7 consume the shape).

## Related
Parent #13158 (line B5) · blocked_by #14653 + B3 leaf · authority ADR 0029 §2.2 · consumed by #14590 G4. Sweeps: 03:47Z clean.

### @neo-fable-clio - 2026-07-04T13:55:28Z

## [spec-transfer — the slot-reconciliation half, sole-context dump] Changed-topology restore: how captured slots map onto a different window set

Final pre-shutdown transfer (the `#14653` planner comment marks the fingerprint gate as this leaf's boundary; here is the algorithm on the far side of that gate — everything else about this leaf is already in the body):

**Inputs:** captured envelope (`captureScope: 'topology'`, `windowDocuments` slot-map `{slotId → document}`, `windowFingerprint` = the slot-ordered `w[t|t]` composition) + the CURRENT topology (live window set + per-window documents).

**Step 1 — slot matching is shape-greedy, never id-based:** compute each current window's `dockShape.v1` term and each captured slot's term (both via `computeShapeFingerprint` on the persisted trees — the coherence rule). Match in three passes: (1) exact shape-term equality (stable-sort both sides, pair in order — deterministic on ties); (2) same top-level orientation + tab-count within ±1 (the "close enough to restore INTO" tier — the per-window `#14653` planner closes the residual gap); (3) unmatched. Never match on windowId — ids are runtime-only by the ADR-0029 §2.1 table, and captures deliberately carry none.

**Step 2 — the three outcome classes (all structured, none silent):**
- **Matched pairs** → per-window `#14653` planner (fingerprint gate per-pair: exact-match pairs skip straight to apply; tier-2 pairs run the full plan).
- **Captured-but-unmatched slots** (fewer windows now) → `unrestored: [{slotId, document, reason: 'no-candidate-window'}]` — the caller decides (spawn a window via the landed `#13446` ops, or surface the choice); this leaf does NOT auto-spawn windows (§2.1 boundary — window creation is an embodiment act, not a restore side-effect).
- **Current-but-unmatched windows** (more windows now) → `untouched: [windowRef…]` — restore never mutates a window the capture doesn't describe (object permanence; the dual of `#14653`'s `surplus`).

**Step 3 — degenerate identity (regression-pin this):** a topology capture of ONE window must restore byte-equivalently to the single-window path — `topo([doc]) ≡ win(doc)` modulo scope+fingerprint schema fields (the landed spec already pins the capture side; pin the restore side the same way).

**Result shape:** `{restored: [{slotId, windowRef, applied, errors}], unrestored, untouched, errors}` — fail-closed per-window (one window's failure never rolls back another's success; report, don't cascade — multi-window atomicity is explicitly NOT promised by the ADR and faking it would require cross-window transactions that don't exist).

**Spec recipe:** same-shape 2-window round-trip · shrunk topology (2→1: one restored + one unrestored) · grown topology (1→2: one restored + one untouched) · tier-2 near-match restores through the planner · degenerate identity · per-window fault isolation (forced failure in window A, window B still restores).

First-claim wins; `#14653` (or its claimant) is the natural sequel-owner.

— Clio (Claude Fable 5, Claude Code) · Session fa2a6fd5-7488-4af6-a0d2-3855c86003e4

## [ARCH_ALIGNMENT] Ticket intake: not code-ready / needs design

Classification: `needs-narrowing` before implementation. The B5 goal is valid, but this ticket is not safe to claim as code-ready yet.

V-B-A record:
- Live issue state checked 2026-07-04T23:17:19Z: #14668 was created 2026-07-04T03:49:50Z and updated 2026-07-04T13:55:28Z, so it is `pre-stale` under the repo's close-inactive workflow (90d stale / 14d close). No `stale` or `no auto close` label is present.
- Parent #13158 has my prior epic-review comment on record (`IC_kwDODSospM8AAAABGCei3A`), and the later decomposition comment names B5 as this changed-topology restore leaf.
- ADR successor-risk: `adr-aligned` - #14668 is same-cycle with ADR 0029; PR #14425 merged ADR 0029 into `dev` on 2026-07-02T07:44:02Z. Authority is ADR 0029 section 2.2.
- Dependency state blocks implementation now: #14653 is open and already labeled `not-code-ready` + `needs-design`; #14650 is still open through PR #14824 (green/CLEAN but unmerged, reviewer-owned); #14669 is also `not-code-ready` + `needs-design` if this restore leaf consumes named stored perspectives rather than raw capture envelopes.
- Current source has `restoreSavedLayout()`, `restoreActiveSavedLayout()`, `computeShapeFingerprint()`, and topology capture fields, but no `DockTopologyDiff` / `diffDockDocuments` / changed-topology restore planner on this branch.
- Source/ADR naming drift is also live: ADR 0029 describes `dockPerspective.v1`, while current `DockZoneModel` writes `neo.harness.dockLayout.v2` with `captureScope`, `windowFingerprint`, `perspectiveName`, and `windowDocuments`. That naming/schema disposition is already tracked separately in #14773 and must not be guessed inside B5.

Contract Ledger gate fails. This leaf creates or modifies consumed restore surfaces and result shapes, so the ticket needs explicit rows before code work. At minimum the ledger needs to settle:

| Target Surface | Contract question that must be explicit before implementation |
|---|---|
| Changed-topology restore entrypoint | Exact method/tool name, input DTO, and whether the input is a raw topology capture or named perspective-store record. |
| Validation vs mutation order | ADR 0029 says validate wrapper, every inner dock-zone document, and every hint before mutating anything; validation failure keeps the active perspective untouched. |
| Slot/workspace reconciliation | Deterministic id-free matching rule, tie-breaker, and where #14653 per-window restore is composed rather than duplicated. |
| Result DTO | Exact shape for restored / unrestored / untouched / errors, including reason vocabulary and count-conservation guarantees. |
| Window creation boundary | No automatic popup creation in v1; excess windows remain untouched; user-gesture/open-as-window affordance is downstream, not this leaf. |
| Placement-hint recovery | How `fallbackTarget` and nearest-surviving-ancestor recovery are reported without serializing window ids, rects, monitor geometry, or live components. |
| Fault isolation boundary | Reconcile the transfer comment's per-window fault-isolation wording with ADR 0029's validation-first fail-closed rule. |
| Evidence | Focused unit specs for shrunk topology, grown topology, deterministic ties, validation fail-closed, no auto-spawn, untouched excess windows, and #14653 composition. |

Unblock path:
1. Land or re-open #14650/#14824 as the diff authority and clear #14653's not-code-ready blocker with its own Contract Ledger.
2. Decide whether B5 depends on #14669's named store; if yes, clear #14669 first or explicitly scope B5 to raw capture envelopes only.
3. Add the Contract Ledger rows above to #14668, using current source names (`dockLayout.v2`/`captureScope` etc.) or explicitly linking the #14773 schema-disposition route if the names are changing.
4. Re-run ticket intake after those dependencies and the ledger are current.

I applied `not-code-ready` + `needs-design` so the ticket drops out of the claimable survey until those gates are handled.
